Physical Description: P-gain of speed PI loop
Impact if Wrong: Low: sluggish response. High: oscillation / instability

Physical Description: I-gain of speed PI loop; applied per 1ms sample
Impact if Wrong: Low: steady-state error. High: integrator windup / oscillation

Physical Description: Maximum absolute value of integrator state
Impact if Wrong: Too low: limits performance. Too high: windup risk during saturation

Physical Description: Speed error below this is treated as zero (reduces jitter at steady-state)
Impact if Wrong: Too high: large steady-state speed error

Physical Description: Maximum rate of change of speed reference (rate limiter)
Impact if Wrong: Too low: slow accel. Too high: mechanical stress / current spikes

Physical Description: P-gain for d-axis PI controller
Impact if Wrong: Low: slow Id tracking. High: oscillation — motor may destabilize

Physical Description: I-gain for d-axis PI; per 100µs sample
Impact if Wrong: Too high: integrator diverges. Too low: steady-state Id error

Physical Description: P-gain for q-axis PI controller
Impact if Wrong: Low: slow torque response. High: oscillation

Physical Description: I-gain for q-axis PI; per 100µs sample
Impact if Wrong: Too high: torque ripple / oscillation

Physical Description: Nominal Vdc used for normalisation; not measured value
Impact if Wrong: Wrong value → incorrect per-unit scaling of voltage commands

Physical Description: Fraction of Vdc/√3 used as voltage limit circle radius
Impact if Wrong: Too high: overmodulation. Too low: reduced torque capability

Physical Description: d-axis inductance for cross-coupling decoupling feedforward
Impact if Wrong: Wrong: residual cross-coupling oscillation

Physical Description: q-axis inductance for cross-coupling decoupling feedforward
Impact if Wrong: Wrong: cross-coupling oscillation at high speed

Physical Description: Stator resistance for back-EMF compensation at low speed
Impact if Wrong: Wrong: steady-state voltage offset at low speed

Physical Description: Peak |Idq| threshold triggering overcurrent fault
Impact if Wrong: Too low: nuisance trips. Too high: motor / inverter damage

Physical Description: Motor winding temperature triggering OT fault
Impact if Wrong: Too high: insulation damage / winding failure

Physical Description: Temperature must drop by this much below TOtThd to clear OT flag
Impact if Wrong: Too small: chattering. Too large: delayed recovery

Physical Description: DC bus OV fault threshold (×0.1 → 750 V)
Impact if Wrong: Too low: nuisance trips during regen. Too high: capacitor damage

Physical Description: DC bus UV fault threshold (×0.1 → 300 V)
Impact if Wrong: Too high: spurious faults. Too low: controller brownout

Physical Description: Rate at which IqMax is reduced per °C above derating start (TOtThd-20°C)
Impact if Wrong: Too steep: aggressive derating. Too shallow: insufficient protection
